decision: The Go codebase owns browser-facing contract types after conversion. TypeScript contract files used by avmatrix-web are generated browser-side adapter artifacts only.

owns: graph node and relationship payload structs
HTTP request and response payload structs
analyze/embed job status and progress structs
session status and stream event structs
forbidden: using avmatrix-shared TypeScript as runtime contract authority after Go cutover
hand-editing generated Web UI TypeScript contract files
letting Web UI generated types import Node-only packages
adding browser-only fields to Go runtime contracts without an explicit adapter mapping

generatedArtifacts.adapterPurpose: Type-only browser compilation surface for avmatrix-web. It must not own runtime behavior.

generationRules: Go structs and constants are the source of truth.
The schema manifest is generated from Go contracts.
The TypeScript adapter is generated from the schema manifest.
Generated files must include a generated-file header and must not be manually edited.
CI must verify that generated files are current before cutover.
compatibilityRules: Generated schema must remain compatible with baseline/phase-1-contract-freeze/web-ui-shared-contract.json.
Field names and JSON casing must stay identical to the current Web UI contract unless a recorded compatibility migration exists.
SSE event names must remain stable.
General HTTP errors must remain {error:string}; session errors must remain {code,error,details?}.
Graph JSON and NDJSON modes must both stay supported.
migrationSequence: Add contract generation command for schema manifest and TypeScript adapter.
Switch avmatrix-web imports from avmatrix-shared runtime contracts to generated adapter types.
Run existing Web UI against Go backend and compare payload compatibility to the Phase 1 baseline.
